t_ch: 希望那位汉密尔顿先生这次能直击重点，别再写些没头没尾的东西了…赶紧、赶紧，趁着我还没晕过去，我们看看线索……
t_en: I hope that Mr. Hamilton will get to the point this time, and stop beating around the bush with his entries... Hurry. Before I faint, we must look at the clues...

t_ch: 我猜他是个假面愚者。
t_en: I'm guessing he's a Masked Fool.

t_ch: 刷盘子确实不容怠慢。
t_en: You can never be too wary of dishwashers.

t_ch: 汉密尔顿…这家伙，不管你现在在哪……
t_en: Hamilton... You annoying fool, no matter where you are right now...

t_ch: 我…我……
<size=36>我要打烂你的脸！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>I'll punch your face in!!!</size>

t_ch: 皮皮西人被那张纸条气得七窍生烟——绝非修辞，而是字面意义上的「冒烟」——然后仰面朝天撅了过去。
t_en: The Pepeshi person is fuming with rage at the note — not a figure of speech, but literally steaming with smoke — and then she turns her face skywards and falls over.

t_ch: 这片梦中的伤心地…你猜她一时半会儿应该不会想再回来了。
t_en: This heart-wrenching land of dreams... You guess that she won't be coming back for a while.

t_ch: 哟，你回来啦？
t_en: Ho, you're back?

t_ch: …您好，{F#小姐}{M#先生}。上次让您看到了我失态的样子，实在是抱歉。
t_en: ...Hello. I must apologize for displaying that misconduct with you the last time.

t_ch: 咳，我得承认，上次调查的*失败*的确给了我不小的打击……
t_en: Ahem, I do have to admit, the failure of the last investigation really hit me hard...

t_ch: 但是作为「真相的仆人」，我——一位敬业的调查记者——是绝对不会轻言放弃的。
t_en: As a servant of truth, I — a committed investigative reporter — will never abandon my creed so easily.

t_ch: 等我再次抓到家族的把柄，{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们还可以再次联手，彻底粉碎家族的阴谋。
t_en: Wait till I dig up more dirt on The Family, {F#miss}{M#mister}. We'll join forces again and shred The Family's conspiracies once and for all.

t_ch: 辛格勒头顶的绒球竖了起来，脸上摆出一幅势在必得的表情。她的执着和自信获得了你的赞许。
t_en: The fluffy ball on Singler's head stands upright, her face a visage of determination. Her resolve and confidence have earned your commendation.

t_ch: 挑战
t_en: Challenge

t_ch: 你找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: Did you want something?

t_ch: 喔…没想到你就是编导口中的「场外嘉宾」。
t_en: Oh... I didn't expect you to be the "special guest" the director mentioned.

t_ch: 咳咳，你好，我是乌玛，家族的新闻主持人，最近匹诺康尼电视台陷入了一点…混乱的局面，编导让我来求助一位「场外嘉宾」，没想到原来是你。
t_en: *ahem* Hi, I'm Uma, a news anchor from The Family. Penacony TV has been caught in a bind lately, so the director sent me to ask for help from a "special guest." I didn't expect it to be you.

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's the issue?

t_ch: 哎…我们现在都没法正常工作，所以…我们想拜托你尽快找出幕后黑手。
t_en: Alas... we're unable to work as usual, so... please help us figure out who's behind the issue as soon as possible.

t_ch: 是要我上节目吗？
t_en: Do I have to appear on the show?

t_ch: 不，我很抱歉，我们不是这个意思。
t_en: No, I'm sorry, but that's not the kind of help we need.

t_ch: 而且…眼下这个情况，电视台的传输信号被干扰了，直播是不可能的。你没看到现在路边的屏幕上放的都是些无限循环的录播画面吗？
t_en: Also, live broadcasting is currently unavailable due to transmission signal interruption. Haven't you noticed we're playing recorded footage on a loop on the roadside billboards?

t_ch: 注意到那些路边的屏幕了吗？家族的技术人员在努力抢修了，但还是有少量屏幕的信号源没能恢复正常……
t_en: Have you noticed the roadside billboards? The technicians from The Family have pulled out all the stops to fix the signal issue, but a handful of screens are still yet to be restored...

t_ch: 好吧，简单来说就是这么回事——电视台的信号被不明团体劫持了，它们自称是什么「惊梦电视台」，接连开了好几档节目。
t_en: To put it simply, Penacony TV's signal has been hijacked by an unknown organization. They called themselves the "Dreamjolt TV" and have aired several TV programs.

t_ch: 他们还扬言要揭露家族的丑行，戳穿匹诺康尼电视台捏造出来的假象——唔，实际上都是些恶心观众的内容。
t_en: They threatened to expose The Family's scandals and debunked the illusions fabricated by Penacony TV. In reality, the content they have shown so far has been nothing but disgusting crap.

t_ch: 编导说你有过人之处，所以台里想请你帮个忙，帮我们查出劫持电视台信号的幕后黑手，让匹诺康尼的电视网络回归正常！
t_en: The director said you were exceptionally talented. We need your help nabbing the culprit who has hijacked the station's signal and restoring the network to normal!

t_ch: 所以我该怎么做？
t_en: What do I need to do?

t_ch: 放心吧，这件事很简单。
t_en: Don't worry. This thing is simple.

t_ch: 感觉好麻烦，我能拒绝吗？
t_en: Sounds like a lot of trouble. Can I say no?

t_ch: 帮个忙嘛！这事很简单，不会耽误你太久的。
t_en: Please do us a favor! It's simple and won't take up too much of your time.

t_ch: 我们已经定位到几处信号异常的终端了。现在，我们只需要一位本领高强的帮手前往那些地点，手动「清除」那些敌台的信号。
t_en: We've located several terminals with anomalous signals. Now, we need someone skilled to visit those spots and remove those signals manually.

t_ch: 在把这几处终端的信号纠正过来之后，我们台里就能定位到真正的幕后黑手——当然，最后这一步也要劳驾你出手解决了。
t_en: After fixing the signals from these terminals, we'll be able to find out the real mastermind behind this incident. Of course, we'll also entrust you with this final step.

t_ch: 听起来不过是一球棒的事。
t_en: Sounds like something that can be done in a jiffy.

t_ch: 我渴望战斗！
t_en: I thirst for battle!

t_ch: 嗯嗯，不愧是「场外嘉宾」，说话就是硬气。
t_en: I admire your confidence.

t_ch: 对了，根据家族送来的情报，我们发现了一个有趣的现象——
t_en: By the way, we've noticed something interesting from the intel sent by The Family.

t_ch: 在和它们交手的时候，找准它们的弱点，就能夺取特定观众的收视率，这会让你在战斗中占据优势。
t_en: When you're fighting with them, you can focus on their weaknesses to gain more audience ratings. This will give you an edge in the battles.

t_ch: 虽然编导说你身手了得，可能用不着这些旁门左道…但有机会的话还是建议你试试，但说不定还能为你减轻点负担呢。
t_en: The director thought you might not need the tip since you were so well-trained... but I'd suggest that you give it a try. It can probably make things easier for you.

t_ch: 那么，电视台的事就拜托你了，「场外嘉宾」{F#小姐}{M#先生}。
t_en: Well, we're counting on you to rescue the station, {F#miss}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 屏幕上播放着某款苏乐达汽水的广告，尽管你分不清它究竟是哪种口味的，但直觉告诉你似乎有什么地方不对劲。
t_en: An advertisement for SoulGlad is playing on the billboard. You cannot tell which flavor it is, but your instinct tells you something is off.

t_ch: （手动换台）
t_en: (Switch the channel manually)

t_ch: 你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目。屏幕闪烁了片刻，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
t_en: You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.

t_ch: 「这是哪个台？这是什么节目？」你感到疑惑。
t_en: "What channel is this? What's this show?" you wonder.

t_ch: 「██味苏乐达独家冠名播出！惊梦电视台王牌舞蹈节目：与猩共舞！」屏幕里传来节目的广告声，你不明白究竟什么人爱看这种节目。
t_en: "This show is sponsored exclusively by ██ SoulGlad! The Dreamjolt TV presents to you our primetime dancing show: Dancing with the Simians!" The introduction of the TV program blares from the screen. You have no idea what kind of audience this show targets.

t_ch: 今晚的主题是！香蕉皮之夜！
t_en: Our theme for tonight is "Night of the Banana Peel"!

t_ch: 你，进来给我跳舞！
t_en: Hey, you. Come on inside and dance!

t_ch: 给它点教训。
t_en: (Show them what you've got.)

t_ch: 屏幕上播放着…一个垃圾袋的特写？你揉了揉眼睛，思忖这会不会是户外垃圾桶的推销广告。
t_en: There is... a close-up of a bin liner on the screen. You rub your eyes, wondering if this might be an advertisement for outdoor trashcans.

t_ch: 镜头在娇小怯弱的垃圾与通体金黄的垃圾之间不断切换——好吧，你承认它曼妙的表皮对你的确很有吸引力，但你还是努力克制住自己升腾的欲望。
t_en: The camera keeps switching back and forth between a small bag of timid trash and another bag that is golden in color. You can't help being drawn to its dazzling appearance, but you make a conscious effort to control the emerging temptation.

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: （手动换台）
t_en: (Switch the channel manually)

t_ch: 你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
t_en: You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.

t_ch: 「诶？那袋金光闪闪的垃圾呢？怎么不播了？」你感到疑惑。
t_en: "Eh? Where's that golden bag of trash? Why don't they show it anymore?" you wonder.

t_ch: 「我就是吃这些东西长大～纯天然，有机～」拿腔拿调的声线将重音停留在「有机」这个词上，每个字结尾都夹杂着古怪的嘶嘶声，令你几欲作呕。
t_en: "I grew up eating these! I'm all natural and organic!" The affected voice emphasizes "organic" and ends every word with an odd hissing sound that makes you feel sick.

t_ch: 随后画面上出现了一个身形健硕的怪人，它自称「银袋山」，是「垃圾家系」的家主，也是惊梦电视台人气真人秀「与银袋山同行」的主角。
t_en: Then, a bizarre, muscular figure shows up on the screen. He introduces himself as Bagtrashian, the head of the Trashian Family. He's also the protagonist of "Keeping Up With the Bagtrashians," a popular reality show by Dreamjolt TV.

t_ch: 嘶～这身腱子肉我没有训练过～我们家天生就这样～怎么～你不相信吗？
t_en: Grrr... I don't have to go to the gym for this body! We're all born this way! You don't believe me?

t_ch: 别骗人了！
t_en: Stop bragging!

t_ch: 明显是后期加工的…
t_en: Clearly, it's post-production special effects...

t_ch: 这又不是我的错～你不相信啊～那你跟我一起上节目，这期就拍你了～
t_en: This isn't my fault! If you don't believe me, come on and join the show! You'll be the star of this episode!

t_ch: 给它点教训。
t_en: (Show them what you've got.)

t_ch: 屏幕上，一顶厨师帽吸引了你的注意力，它看来是如此地平凡又普通。但你知道，越是普通的东西越危险——
t_en: A chef's hat on the screen catches your eye. There seems to be nothing special about it, but you know that sometimes the more inconspicuous a thing is, the more dangerous it can be.

t_ch: （手动换台）
t_en: (Switch the channel manually)

t_ch: 你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
t_en: You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.

t_ch: 「美食节目还能有什么幺蛾子？」你感到疑惑。
t_en: "What kind of surprises could there be in a gourmet show?" you doubt.

t_ch: 一个戴着厨师帽怪模怪样的恐龙出现在电视画面中，它咧开血盆大口用力地嘶吼着，每句话都像在狠狠地喷人。
t_en: A curious-looking dinosaur, donning a chef's hat, makes a most peculiar entrance. With a gaping maw, he shouts and yells. Each of his utterances spews forth with such ferocity and a cutting acidity that seems to scorch the very souls of his audience.

t_ch: 主厨喜欢用嘴巴里的火来喷人——然而这却是这档「地狱厨房」节目的名场面。
t_en: The head chef seems to enjoy the spectacle of spewing fire from his mouth at people, which is, incidentally, the main selling point of this "Inferno's Kitchen" show.

t_ch: 这根本不是人吃的东西！
t_en: I wouldn't feed this to my worst enemy!

t_ch: 因为…这是我这种纵横厨界几十年的老家伙，才有资格品尝的菜肴。
t_en: That is because... it's a kaleidoscope of gastronomic delectation, which only a proper geezer like me, a master chef with decades of culinary experience under the belt, is entitled to savor.

t_ch: 好！大！一！块！生！肉！就让我来烤烤你！
t_en: IT'S RAW!!!!!! WHAT! A! DREADFUL! CHUNK! OF! RAW! MEAT! Do I need to put you on a grill!?

t_ch: 主厨兼主持人将视线转向你，酝酿着喉间的火焰——
t_en: The chef-cum-host turns his eyes to you and readies the flames in his throat...

t_ch: 给它点教训。
t_en: (Show them what you've got.)

t_ch: 屏幕中正在播放一档名为「匹诺康尼偶像」的选秀节目，一位选手真诚地笑着等待评委给出结果。
t_en: A talent show titled "Penaconian Idol" is playing on the screen. A contestant is waiting for the evaluation result with a sincere smile.

t_ch: 节目保留了评判结果的悬念，镜头在职业观众的假笑与愤怒之间来回切换，令你感到不适，你决定——
t_en: To keep the audience in suspense, the camera alternates between phony smiles and irritated faces of the professional audience. Feeling perturbed, you decide to...

t_ch: （手动换台）
t_en: (Switch the channel manually)

t_ch: 你试图换台，播放乌玛和她的同事们录制好的节目，但屏幕闪烁了片刻后，却没等来你想要的频道与画面。
t_en: You try switching the channel to play the pre-recorded show done by Uma and her colleagues. The screen flickers for a while, but the channel you want still doesn't come on.

t_ch: 「什么情况？」你感到疑惑。
t_en: "What?" You're puzzled.

t_ch: 负分！晋级失败！
t_en: The contestant is eliminated!

t_ch: 会唱歌有什么用，你都没有嚎啕大哭，也没有讲述你的悲惨身世。没有节目效果我怎么让你过？
t_en: It's not enough to just sing well. Without tearing up or narrating the story of your miserable life, you add nothing to the show. How can I let you through?

t_ch: 刁钻的评委将视线转向你。
t_en: The crafty judge shifts their gaze toward you.

t_ch: 下一位选手——{NICKNAME}，是吧？说吧，你的梦想是什么？
t_en: Next up! {NICKNAME}, right? Tell us about your dream.

t_ch: 给它点教训。
t_en: (Show them what you've got.)

t_ch: 在赶走了入侵电视台的四位「惊梦电视台」成员后，家族排除到了幕后黑手所在的位置。
t_en: After getting rid of the four Dreamjolt TV employees who have invaded the station, The Family has located the mastermind.

t_ch: 这片组合屏幕可真够大的…你有点看不懂这究竟是什么图案。
t_en: This is a massive spliced screen... There is a pattern on it that you don't recognize.

t_ch: 就在你靠近屏幕时，眼前的画面却突然模糊了起来。你头晕目眩，眼前闪过许多节目的画面，耳畔同时响起的几个嘈杂的声音还在不断讨论着什么……
t_en: As you edge closer to the screen, the image on it suddenly blurs, leaving your head spinning. The scenes of multiple TV programs are flashing before you, and you can hear a clamorous discussion going on nearby...

t_ch: 你冷静下来，深深地吸了几口气，抬头看，却发现一道可怖的身影出现在屏幕上。
t_en: You calm yourself down by taking a few deep breaths. By the time you look up again, you find a fearful figure emerging on the screen.

t_ch: 这究竟是什么？你不理解，也不敢多看。
t_en: What exactly is that? You have no clue and dare not linger your eyes on it.

t_ch: 你短促地抬头望了一眼，那些聒噪的声画便再度出现了。你瞬间感到心烦无比，只觉得莫名地升腾起一肚子怒火——
t_en: You cast a fleeting glance at it, and soon the cacophonous scene returns, driving you up the wall...

t_ch: 给它一棒！
t_en: Give it a whack!

t_ch: 嘿，那边的——
t_en: Hey there...

t_ch: 之前不是都见过面了？别太拘谨，放松。这里是冷清了点，不过正好，适合无所顾忌地谈话。
t_en: Didn't we already meet before? You should loosen up a bit. The place is quiet, but that's okay — we can say whatever we want here.

t_ch: 后来你跟加拉赫聊得还愉快吧？别看他老是漫不经心的样子，心里头埋着的事可多了。要不然，也不会成为这家酒吧的常客。
t_en: Did your conversation with Gallagher last time go well? He might look like he doesn't care about anything, but he actually has a lot on his mind. Otherwise, he wouldn't be a regular here.

t_ch: 我的酒吧别的没有，快乐可还真不少。
t_en: My place offers nothing but joy.

t_ch: 直接说正事吧。
t_en: Let's cut to the chase.

t_ch: 噢，原来你是直奔主题的类型？单纯点是好事，但太直接有时会不太讨喜哦？
t_en: Oh, I see you like to be straightforward. It's good to keep things simple, but you might get on people's nerves for being too direct.

t_ch: …开个玩笑，我就喜欢像你这样务实的个性。
t_en: ...I'm kidding. I like how pragmatic you are.

t_ch: 把我喊出来就为了说这个啊？
t_en: Did you ask me out just to tell me that?

t_ch: 那你想跟我聊什么呢？
t_en: What do you want to talk about, then?

t_ch: 聊天不过是拉近关系的方式，只要达到了目的，具体聊什么并不重要，不是吗？
t_en: Chatting is just a way to get closer to each other. As long as the conversation fulfills its purpose, it doesn't matter what we actually talk about. Don't you think so?

t_ch: 好啦，我猜你差不多也放松点了。特意请你过来，确实是有工作需要帮忙。
t_en: Alright, you seem more relaxed now. I invited you here because I needed your help.

t_ch: 见识过加拉赫的本事，不知道你对调饮这门手艺有没有更感兴趣——实不相瞒，我有些临时的事务缠身，得离开酒吧一段时间，这里缺个临时顶班的调饮师。
t_en: Are you interested in the art of mixing drinks, especially after seeing how well Gallagher does it? Something unexpected has come up, and I need to be away for a while. I'm looking for someone to take my place temporarily.

t_ch: 我觉得你很有做这行的天分，想请你替我顶会儿班。有兴趣吗？
t_en: I think you've got the talent to ace the job. What do you say?

t_ch: 为什么会选我呢？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 直觉。再说了，看中一个人需要理由吗？
t_en: I just go with my gut. Must I have a reason for that?

t_ch: 你可算是找对人了。
t_en: You've picked the right person.

t_ch: 我的眼光向来不会出错，你肯定能成为相当优秀的调饮师。
t_en: I've always had a good eye for talent, and I believe you'll be an awesome drinksmith.

t_ch: 来，先试试吧。别紧张，我会手把手教你的。
t_en: Come on and try it. Don't be nervous, I'll guide you step by step.

t_ch: 教教我。
t_en: Show me how.

t_ch: 稍微教教就会，挺不错嘛。技术方面算是合格了。
t_en: You're getting the hang of things already! Technique-wise, you're a pass.

t_ch: 是你教的好。
t_en: It's only because you teach well.

t_ch: 事先说清楚，现在奉承我可没有奖励——当然了，我的心情确实会变好。
t_en: Just to be clear, flattery will get you nowhere. It does make my mood better though.

t_ch: 难道我真是个天才？
t_en: Am I really a genius?

t_ch: 别着急，我要说的才刚刚开始呢。
t_en: Don't get ahead of yourself. We're just getting started.

t_ch: 技术是成为调饮师的必备条件，但想要调出真正让人满意的饮品，需要的远远不止这个。
t_en: Knowing the mixing technique is the prerequisite to becoming a drinksmith. However, it takes more than that to serve drinks that impress your guests.

t_ch: 优秀的调饮师所需要具备的、最重要的技能，是聆听——<color=#dbc291ff>调饮…就是与客人对话的过程。</color>
t_en: The most crucial skill that a good drinksmith needs is listening... <color=#dbc291ff>Drinksmithing... is all about having conversations with your guests.</color>

t_ch: 饮品是种神奇的东西。就凭这一小杯漂亮的液体，辅以舒适的氛围、再加上一点点聊天技巧…它就能轻易敲开对方的心扉，毫不费力。
t_en: Drinks are magical. A beautiful glass of liquid, a cozy ambiance, and a little bit of conversational skills are all you need to make someone open up.

t_ch: 听起来有些玄乎，但我相信你拥有这样的能力。我早就看出来了，你是个善于共情的人…不仅如此，你能看透客人们的内心，甚至*调整*他们的情绪。
t_en: This might sound a bit esoteric, but I believe you have the knack for it. I can tell you're someone with empathy... Not only that, but you can also read someone's mind and even *regulate* their emotions.

t_ch: 你怎么知道我有超能力的？
t_en: How did you find out about my power?

t_ch: 在这里工作得久了，自然会懂得观察。理解顾客可是每位调饮师的必修课。
t_en: I've learned to pick things up from my experience here. Being able to understand the guests is a must for every drinksmith.

t_ch: 喂喂，你这也太抬举我了。
t_en: You're flattering me.

t_ch: 呵。那就当是我言过其实吧，但你确实很擅长和人交流，不愿意承认吗？
t_en: Well, I might be exaggerating things, but I'm sure you know how good you are at connecting with people.

t_ch: 上次我都看到了，你和艾米绮聊得很开心。她的性格我最清楚，黏人得很，让她安静离开可不容易。所以，我相信你能胜任这份工作。
t_en: I saw you having a good time talking to Amiky last time. I know her well — she can be clingy, and it's not easy to convince her to leave without making a fuss. So, I believe you're the right fit for the job.

t_ch: 怎么样，不说话就当你答应了哦？
t_en: What do you say? I'll take your silence as a yes.

t_ch: 我不说话。
t_en: I'll stay silent then.

t_ch: 哈哈，满分的幽默感。不错不错，你果然很适合做调饮师！
t_en: Haha, you've got a great sense of humor. You're really cut out for this!

t_ch: 我有异议！
t_en: I disagree!

t_ch: 要是你真的一点兴趣没有，那又是什么驱使你来到这里的呢？多余的感情？
t_en: If you aren't into mixing drinks in the least bit, what made you come here? Your overflowing sentiments?

t_ch: 哈哈哈。既然都跟我学了调饮，就没有让你离开的选择了。安心留在这吧。
t_en: Hahaha. Since you've decided to learn from me, there's no backing out now. Just chill out and make yourself at home here.

t_ch: …在正式开始工作前，有件事必须告诉你。这家酒吧——正式名字叫「惊梦酒吧」——确实有它的特别之处。
t_en: ...Before we start, I need to give you a heads-up. The name of this place is "Dreamjolt Hostelry," and there's something unique about it.

t_ch: 这间酒吧的顾客并非匹诺康尼的宾客或逐梦客，而是<color=#dbc291ff>生活在梦境酒店里的怪物。</color>
t_en: At this lounge, we don't serve the guests or Dreamchaser in Penacony, but the <color=#dbc291ff>monsters staying in the hotel in the dreamscape.</color>

t_ch: 它们原本是为家族服务的美梦剧团成员，但…我想你应该知道了，这片梦境正在坍塌，它们受到负面情绪的熏染，脾性发生改变，成为了难以控制的怪物。
t_en: They used to be the members of the Sweet Dreams Troupe that served The Family... As you should know, the Dreamscape is collapsing. The negative emotions have altered the temperament of the troupe members, turning them into uncontrollable monsters.

t_ch: 我留在这里，就是为了帮助它们稳定情绪，恢复到原本的模样。这是个漫长的过程，如果问题得不到解决，它们会重新陷入狂乱。我不想看到这种事发生。
t_en: I stay here to help them stabilize their emotions and restore them to their original form, which can be a long process. If the issue isn't resolved, they'll revert to the frenzied state, which is the last thing I want to see.

t_ch: 我不在的时候，这些怪物就要拜托你了。
t_en: Please take care of the monsters when I'm not around.

t_ch: 原来你藏了个大的啊。
t_en: So there's a catch.

t_ch: 需要做的事情都差不多，也算不上是欺骗吧？
t_en: Since your job scope is more or less the same, it doesn't count as fraud, does it?

t_ch: 我是怪物保护协会的成员。
t_en: I'm a member of the Monster Protection Society.

t_ch: 有这种协会么？不介意的话，让他们来我这团建呗，我正好也见见。
t_en: Is there such a society? If you don't mind, you can invite your fellow members here for team building. I'd love to meet them.

t_ch: 当然，那些怪物没什么攻击性，不会伤害你，只是…性格上有点麻烦，需要多点耐心。
t_en: Well, those monsters aren't aggressive, so they won't hurt you. It's just that... their personalities can be a little abrasive, so you need to be more patient.

t_ch: 它们的具体情况都记在这本<color=#dbc291ff>问题手册</color>里，你可以翻翻看，在接待过程中有什么需要备忘的，也可以随时写进去。
t_en: You can find details about their conditions in this <color=#dbc291ff>Troubleshooter</color>. Feel free to flip through it and jot down stuff in it.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>为怪物们提供需要的饮料，获得信任，进而解决它们的情绪困扰</color>——这就是「惊梦酒吧」的调饮师需要完成的工作。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Making drinks for the monsters to gain their trust and help with their emotional troubles</color> is the job of a drinksmith at Dreamjolt Hostelry.

t_ch: …所以我是心理咨询师？
t_en: ...That makes me a counselor?

t_ch: 哈哈，也可以这么说？和怪物深入交流的机会可不是哪都有的，算是特色了吧。
t_en: Haha, you can put it that way. The chance to have in-depth conversations with monsters doesn't come by every day. Consider it a perk.

t_ch: 交给我的麻烦事真多啊。
t_en: Sounds like I'm taking up quite an onerous task.

t_ch: 哈哈，这不是能者多劳嘛。你不来当这里的调饮师可实在太浪费了。
t_en: Haha, with great talent comes great responsibility! It would be a waste if you didn't work as a drinksmith here.

t_ch: 其实要做的事还是那样，倾听怪物们的需求，为它们提供想要的饮料。可能的话，尽量陪它们多聊聊。它们很怕寂寞。
t_en: Actually, your job is pretty much the same — to listen to the needs of the monsters and provide suitable drinks for them. Try talking to them as much as possible. They really hate to be lonely.

t_ch: 还记得我说的话吗，饮品是种很神奇的东西，能打开对方的心扉。调饮能做到的事，远比你预想得要多。
t_en: Still remember what I said just now? Drinks are magical things that can make someone open up. What a drinksmith can achieve is so much more than you might expect.

t_ch: 好啦，后面就交给你了。「惊梦酒吧」…新来的调饮师？
t_en: Now, I'll leave the Dreamjolt Hostelry under your care...

t_ch: （只接待了两个客人，却憋了一肚子的火！）
t_en: (I'm about to blow a fuse already just after serving two customers!)

t_ch: …感觉怎么样，还顺利吗？
t_en: ...Is everything going alright?

t_ch: …最生气的一集！
t_en: ...This is by far the most infuriating thing!

t_ch: 想狠狠教训它们！
t_en: I feel like teaching them a lesson!

t_ch: 哈哈，刚开始是这样。别太往心里去。怪物们受噩梦的侵袭太深，性格已经变得奇怪了。
t_en: Haha, it's normal to feel that way in the beginning. Please don't take it to heart. They're just too affected by the nightmare that they can be a bit twisted.

t_ch: 在过去，它们的情绪还正常的时候，都是相当乖巧懂事的孩子，很讨人喜欢。
t_en: When things were all good in the past, they were a bunch of lovely and sensible kids.

t_ch: …辛苦了。我先向你表示感谢。
t_en: ...I really appreciate your hard work.

t_ch: 我也没做什么特别的。
t_en: I haven't done much.

t_ch: 能接纳这些怪物，和它们平等交流…我就很感激了。
t_en: I'm already grateful because you can accept these monsters and treat them as equals.

t_ch: 只是听它们说话而已。
t_en: I only lend my ear to them.

t_ch: 你回应得也很不错。能接纳这些怪物，和它们平等交流…我就很感激了。
t_en: And you respond to their issues well. I'm already grateful because you can accept these monsters and treat them as equals.

t_ch: 嗯，是时候教你「进阶课程」了。
t_en: Hmm, it's time to give you the "advanced lesson."

t_ch: 调制饮品讲究创意，需要根据时机与客人们的喜好来制作独特的饮品。
t_en: A creative mind is key to mixing drinks. You need to come up with special blends based on the time and your customer's preferences.

t_ch: 等我把「自由调制」的秘诀传授给你，你就能制作出酒单上不存在的饮品了。这可是每位调饮师的必修课——总给客人喝卖相、成分全都一成不变的饮料，换谁都会腻的。
t_en: After I show you the ropes about "Free Mixing," you'll be able to craft drinks that aren't on the menu. This is something every drinksmith has to learn. Things could get boring if you keep dishing out the same recipes.

t_ch: 比如说，我现在想喝<color=#dbc291ff>大杯特别甜</color>，能够让人回忆起过往幸福时光的饮料——
t_en: For instance, I want a <color=#dbc291ff>large, extra sweet</color> drink that brings back happy memories of my past...

t_ch: 酒单上的品类可满足不了这种需求，你要怎么处理呢？
t_en: Nothing on the menu fits the requirements. What should you do now?

t_ch: 让我想想…
t_en: Hmm... Let me think about it.

t_ch: 别慌，我来教你。
t_en: Don't panic. Let me show you what to do.

t_ch: 相当出色！你对味道的把握很敏锐，同时能准确洞察对方的内心…说不定，有天我都得请你为我调制适合的饮品。
t_en: Excellent! Your grasp of flavors is on point, and you can accurately perceive what's on someone's mind... I might ask you to make a drink for me one day.

t_ch: 后面我要暂时离开，但怪物们还是会定期光顾，酒吧就得拜托你打理了。缺少了这里提供的饮料，我担心它们的情绪会变得不稳定。
t_en: I'll be gone for a while, but the monsters will still drop by from time to time. Please tend the lounge for me. Without the drinks here, I'm worried those monsters might get angsty.

t_ch: 好啦，工作了这么久，你肯定也累了。先去休息吧。
t_en: Now, you must be drained after a long shift. Take a break.

t_ch: （整天接待这种奇奇怪怪的客人还真挺累的，调饮师的工作果然不好做啊。）
t_en: (Dealing with these weirdos day-in, day-out is indeed quite tiring. Being a drinksmith is certainly no easy feat.)

t_ch: （果然舒翁不在店里…直接开始营业吧。）
t_en: (As expected, Siobhan is not around... Let's open for business.)

t_ch: （咦，怎么好像听到有打架的声音……）
t_en: (Eh? Why is there a commotion...)

t_ch: 这是我最珍贵的收音机汪！就被你这么弄坏了。要赔，全赔给我哇！
t_en: I love this radio, woof! You broke it! I want you to pay for it!

t_ch: 噢。抱歉，我不是故意要这么做的，只是…不小心，手劲大了点，就捏碎了哈哈哈！你不会要掉小珍珠了吧？多来点，多来点！
t_en: Oh. I'm sorry, and I didn't mean to break it. I just... held it too tightly and crushed it by accident, hahaha! Are you going to cry? More, please!

t_ch: …怎么回事？
t_en: ...What happened?

t_ch: 汪！我好心把收音机借给荷官先生玩，没想到…它直接就朝地板上摔了哇！
t_en: Woof! I lent my radio to the dealer out of kindness, but he smashed it on the ground!

t_ch: 没有摔，没有摔…准确说，是捏。我就稍微用了点力，它就碎了。太脆弱了，这可不行。
t_en: There was no smashing of any kind... To be more precise, I only pinched it, and it shattered after I applied a little pressure. How fragile.

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎嘎，你看，要是它比地板更硬，是不是不会有事了？对吧！
t_en: Woah, woah, woah. Did you hear him? It wouldn't have happened if the radio was harder than the floor, would it?

t_ch: 呜——你就是存心捉弄我，把你咬成碎片汪！
t_en: *whimper*... You're clearly messing with me. I'll tear you into pieces, woof!

t_ch: 不许打架！
t_en: No fighting!

t_ch: 我是这里的调饮师，都听我的！
t_en: I'm the drinksmith, and I call the shots here!

t_ch: 呜呜…新人，我好委屈，我是冤枉的，你可得帮我评评理呀。
t_en: *sob*... I'm being framed, newcomer. You've got to help me out.

t_ch: 汪！我什么都没做，全部都是荷官先生的错哇！
t_en: Woof! I didn't do anything! It was all his fault!

t_ch: （它们都不承认自己做错了，还是分别去问问究竟发生了什么吧。）
t_en: (Neither of them admits to any wrongdoing. I should probably ask them separately.)

t_ch: 我、我很无辜的呀。谁叫那个收音机…突然发出那么大的噪音，震得我的小心脏生疼生疼的，脚一滑，手一抖，就摔地上了。我也是受害者呜呜。
t_en: I—I'm innocent. That radio just randomly started blaring out loud noise. I was startled and slipped. My grasp on the radio loosened, and then it dropped. *sob* I'm a victim too.

t_ch: 再说嘛，就是个收音机，干嘛这么在乎。想听点啥…嘿，不如，我来讲给它听。
t_en: Besides, it's just a radio. What's the big deal anyway? If he wants to listen to something... Heh, I'll give him something to listen to.

t_ch: 恐怖的搞笑的正经的不适宜播出的，我啥都能讲，哇嘎嘎，尤其擅长讲狗狗的笑话！
t_en: I can talk about all kinds of stuff: Scary, funny, serious, or even the kind of stuff you can't put on the airwaves. Whoa now, I'm also quite the expert when it comes to canine jokes!

t_ch: 去向它道歉。
t_en: Apologize.

t_ch: 你…是认真的吗？你希望…我，如此善良热情的乖孩子，去向那只傻狗低头认错？
t_en: Are you... serious? You want me... an innocent and passionate kid to bow my head and apologize to that idiot pooch?

t_ch: 我好委屈呜呜。我当然知道自己做了什么，也分得清对错，坦率跟你说…这次的事，我都是故意的哈哈哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: I feel so wronged! Of course, I know what I've done. I can tell the difference between right and wrong. To be honest with you... I did it on purpose, hahaha!

t_ch: 来啊，来教训我啊！嘻嘻…我就这个样，打出生就没变过，谁都拿我没辙！
t_en: Bring it on! Show me what's what! Hehe... I've been like this since the day I was born. Nobody can deal with me!

t_ch: （…或许可以趁机进入它的内心世界，帮它改掉这个爱捉弄人的坏毛病。）
t_en: (...Maybe I can seize this chance to slip into his internal world and help him change this bad habit of pranking others.)

t_ch: 来啊，来教训我啊！嘻嘻…我就这个样，打出生就没变过，谁都拿我没辙！
t_en: Bring it on! Show me what's what! Hehe... I've been like this since the day I was born. Nobody can deal with me!

t_ch: （…或许可以趁机进入它的内心世界，帮它改掉这个爱捉弄人的坏毛病。）
t_en: (...Maybe I can seize this chance to slip into his internal world and help him change this bad habit of pranking others.)

t_ch: 好好教训它一顿！
t_en: Teach him a lesson!

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 我就在这等着，看你能拿我怎么办！
t_en: I'll wait right here. Let's see if you can handle me!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎！就喜欢你看不惯我，又拿我没办法的模样！
t_en: Whoa! I love seeing you hate me and not be able to do anything about it!

t_ch: 这是假的哈哈，你被耍啦！
t_en: I got you! You fell for it!

t_ch: 你抓住我了。对、对、对不起！因为我、我…不在那个地方哈哈哈！
t_en: You caught me! I—I'm sorry because I—I... am not there! Hahaha!

t_ch: 放、放过我吧！…我诚挚认错，我真心道歉，我向全世界受欺负的狗狗说对不起！
t_en: L—Let me go! ...I know I was wrong. I'm truly sorry, and I'll apologize to every dog around the world who has ever been bullied!

t_ch: 从现在起，我将成为所有狗狗的守护者，绝不让它们掉眼泪，至、至死方休！
t_en: From this moment on, I will be the protector of all dogs! I will make sure no dog ever cries and will do so until the day I die!

t_ch: 我…我……
你还想再被教训一顿？
t_en: You want me to teach you another lesson?

t_ch: 这就是你的认错态度？
t_en: Is this how you apologize?

t_ch: 我…呜呜……
t_en: I... *sob*

t_ch: 其实…我真不是故意的，不是…舒翁最近总不在嘛，我就想…闹出点动静，说不定能把她喊回来。怪物们出了事，舒翁肯定会赶来。肯定会的。
t_en: Actually... I didn't really do it on purpose. That wasn't it at all... It's just that, well, Siobhan hasn't been around recently. I thought maybe... if I made a scene, she might come back. Siobhan would come rushing back if the monsters were in trouble. I'm sure of it.

t_ch: 所以…就砸了那只傻狗的东西……
t_en: So that's why... I smashed that idiot pooch's thing...

t_ch: 这个不是理由吧？
t_en: That's not a good reason, you know?

t_ch: 这么做也不对吧？
t_en: You shouldn't have done that, right?

t_ch: 我知道…我知道的…后面会注意的，不捣乱不开玩笑不嘲笑那只傻狗。
t_en: I know... I know... From here on out, I'll be sure to not bother or make fun of that idiot pooch.

t_ch: 「傻狗！」——啪！你瞧，多响的耳光，可痛了，痛得我记一辈子都忘不掉！
t_en: "Idiot pooch!" *slap* See how hard that slap was? It hurt so much I'll never forget!

t_ch: 要不，呜呜…这次就先放过我？
t_en: What if, *sniffle*... you let me off this time?

t_ch: 不会再有下次了哦。
t_en: There won't be any second chances.

t_ch: 以后要打十个耳光！
t_en: If this happens again, you'll get slapped ten times!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎嘎！奇迹的复活！
t_en: Whoa whoa whoa! I've been miraculously revived!

t_ch: 我就是个乖孩子~收起那不羁的性子~过着幸福的日子~谁都喜欢骰子呼哈哈哈！
t_en: I'm a good boy~ I'm putting my uninhibited nature behind me~ It's all happy days from here~ Everyone likes dice, haha!

t_ch: 我就是个乖孩子~收起那不羁的性子~过着幸福的日子~谁都喜欢骰子呼哈哈哈！
t_en: I'm a good boy~ I'm putting my uninhibited nature behind me~ It's all happy days from here~ Everyone likes dice, haha!

t_ch: …不是我的错哇！我只是…好心把珍藏的收音机借它玩玩，就直接给我砸了汪！
t_en: ...It's not my fault! All I did was... lend him the radio out of the kindness of my heart. And the next thing I knew, he smashed it, woof!

t_ch: 新来的调饮师，你可得帮我！
t_en: You're the new drinksmith. You've got to help me!

t_ch: 那是很重要的东西吗？
t_en: Was the radio important to you?

t_ch: 汪！那是我最爱的宝物，当然很重要哇！并且…并且……
t_en: Woof! It's my precious treasure! Of course, it's important. And... and...

t_ch: …你真的没有做错吗？
t_en: ...You didn't actually do anything wrong?

t_ch: 没有哇。我只是…稍微多嘲讽了荷官先生几句，笑得大声了点，还说它没有家人……
t_en: I didn't, woof. I did... taunt the dealer a little. I laughed a little loudly and said he didn't have a family...

t_ch: 但它也不能砸坏我的宝物哇，那个很重要。并且…并且……
t_en: But that doesn't mean he can go and smash my treasure! It was so important to me... And... and...

t_ch: 那个收音机是我重要的回忆，是舒翁送给我的。
t_en: That radio represented an important memory for me. Siobhan gave it to me.

t_ch: 最近舒翁总是不在，没人陪我玩，我好寂寞，所以才想和荷官先生出去玩……
t_en: Siobhan hasn't been around recently. There's no one for me to play with. I'm so lonely. That's the only reason I wanted to play with the dealer...

t_ch: 我故意惹人生气，也是想引起注意。不然…都没有人关注我……
t_en: I made him angry on purpose because I wanted him to notice me. If I didn't do that... no one would pay attention to me...

t_ch: 呜呜…至少脾气坏点，自私点，就能过得开心，你还会多和我聊会天……
t_en: *sob*... At the very least, if I behave badly, more selfish, I can be happy. At least you'd talk to me more...

t_ch: （是不是因为没人陪它玩，才会变得这么讨厌…去它的内心世界里看看吧。）
t_en: (Maybe he's become so unbearable because no one is spending time with him... I should go into his internal world and see what's going on.)

t_ch: 最近舒翁总是不在，没人陪我玩，我好寂寞，所以才想和荷官先生出去玩……
t_en: Siobhan hasn't been around recently. There's no one for me to play with. I'm so lonely. That's the only reason I wanted to play with the dealer...

t_ch: 我故意惹人生气，也是想引起注意。不然…都没有人关注我……
t_en: I made him angry on purpose because I wanted him to notice me. If I didn't do that... no one would pay attention to me...

t_ch: 呜呜…至少脾气坏点，自私点，就能过得开心，你还会多和我聊会天……
t_en: *sob*... At the very least, if I behave badly, more selfish, I can be happy. At least you'd talk to me more...

t_ch: （是不是因为没人陪它玩，才会变得这么讨厌…去它的内心世界里看看吧。）
t_en: (Maybe he's become so unbearable because no one is spending time with him... I should go into his internal world and see what's going on.)

t_ch: 安抚它的情绪。
t_en: Soothe its feelings.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 汪！新来的调饮师，别丢下我不管哇……
t_en: Woof! Drinksmith! Don't just cast me to the side...

t_ch: 这边这边！陪我玩！我看到速度快的东西就想扑上去汪！
t_en: Over here, over here! Play with me! I wanna pounce on anything I see moving fast, woof!

t_ch: 汪！！太神奇了！我们继续玩吧！
t_en: Woof!! Incredible! Let's keep playing!

t_ch: 汪汪！！玩得好开心！怎么突然就消失不见了哇！！
t_en: Woof, woof! That was fun! Where'd you go all of a sudden!?

t_ch: 感觉好多了…原来有人陪是件这么幸福的事情汪！
t_en: I feel so much better... Who knew having some company could be such a wonderful thing, woof?

t_ch: 下次还要带我玩好不好！汪汪！想出去玩，想出去玩！
t_en: You gotta take me out to play again, okay? Woof, woof! I wanna go play! I wanna go play!

t_ch: 现在还会故意惹人生气吗？
t_en: Will you intentionally make someone angry again?

t_ch: …汪？我是给你带来幸福的汽水狗狗，聪明又善良，只会讨人喜欢，不会添麻烦的哇！
t_en: ...Woof? I'll be the Soda Doggo that brings you happiness, who's smart and nice. I'll be friendly and won't cause any trouble, woof!

t_ch: 新来的调饮师，以后还要再陪我玩哇！
t_en: Play with me again sometime, drinksmith!

t_ch: 汪！下次带我出去玩好不好？新来的调饮师，以后还要再陪我哇！
t_en: Woof! Take me out to play again, all right? Be sure to hang out with me again, drinksmith!

t_ch: 喂，傻狗！…傻狗？…傻狗傻狗傻狗傻狗哈哈哈！嘴皮子使劲耍，谁都拿我没办法！
t_en: Hey, idiot pooch! ...Idiot pooch? ...Idiot idiot idiot idiot pooch! Hahaha! I've got quite the mouth. No one can handle this!

t_ch: 汪！我坏掉的收音机，你还没赔偿哇！
t_en: Woof! You still haven't paid me back for the radio you broke!

t_ch: 酒吧就这么大，你跑不掉的汪！
t_en: Even if this lounge is big, you won't escape!

t_ch: 呃…要不，你看这样，我带你去酒店房间里转转，那里有好多客人不要的东西。
t_en: Uh... Well, how about this? I'll take you to the hotel room. There's all sorts of stuff in there that guests don't want.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，肯定能找到你想要的！
t_en: Hehe, I'm sure you'll find something you'll like!

t_ch: 会有能用的收音机哇？
t_en: Got a radio that works?

t_ch: 去看看，去看看就知道了。走咯！追上来，不然我就跑咯！
t_en: You'll find out if you go take a look. I'm off! Better keep up, or you'll be eating my dust!

t_ch: 汪！等等我哇！
t_en: Woof! Wait for me!

t_ch: （它们的关系…应该是恢复正常了吧？）
t_en: (Looks like they've... patched things up?)

t_ch: 唷，我回来了，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Hey, I'm back, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 如何？我不在的这段时间，调饮师当得还开心吗？
t_en: How'd it go? Did you like being a drinksmith while I was away?

t_ch: 我很快乐。
t_en: I had a good time.

t_ch: 辛苦了，但你的表情可把你给出卖了。跟我讲讲呗，遇到了什么事？
t_en: Thanks for your hard work, but your face says it all. Tell it to me straight. What happened?

t_ch: 回想起来，尽是悲伤的事。
t_en: All my mind can come up with are sad moments.

t_ch: 你悲伤的样子也很可爱。遇到了什么烦心事，跟我讲讲呗。
t_en: You look pretty cute when you're sad. What problems did you encounter? I'm all ears.

t_ch: 喜悦因分享而加倍，忧伤因倾诉而减半。调饮师的工作，不就是聆听和交流嘛。
t_en: Sharing is so important. It can double our blessings and cut our burdens in half. The work of a drinksmith is all about listening and chatting.

t_ch: （向舒翁讲述发生的事。）
t_en: (Tell Siobhan what happened.)

t_ch: 原来如此。 随着梦境加速坍塌，怪物的情绪越来越不稳定，类似的冲突也在逐渐增加，不知道以后会不会有更麻烦的事。
t_en: So that's what happened. As the collapse of the Dreamscape accelerates, the monsters' emotions become increasingly unstable. Similar conflicts will only keep growing in number. Who knows, maybe we'll have a bigger headache to deal with later.

t_ch: 但你处理得很好，是位合格的调饮师了。我替它们向你表达感谢。
t_en: But you did great. You're a bona fide drinksmith. Allow me to thank you on their behalf.

t_ch: 这也是调饮师的职责啊？
t_en: Is this part of a drinksmith's gig, huh?

t_ch: 作为「惊梦酒吧」的调饮师，处理好怪物间的关系是必修课。我可是在最开始就警告过你的哦？这里的调饮师不是那么好当的。
t_en: As a drinksmith at "Dreamjolt Hostelry," handling the monsters' interpersonal relationships is all part of the job. Didn't I tell you right when you started? Being a drinksmith here isn't a walk in the park.

t_ch: 我自愿做的。
t_en: I took the job voluntarily.

t_ch: 哈哈哈。那就更该好好谢你了。毕竟…敢和怪物们接触的人本就没几个，像你这样愿意用真心对待它们的人就更难找了。
t_en: Hahaha. Then I really should thank you. After all... there aren't many people who'd dare work with monsters, and even fewer who'd be willing to treat them with sincerity like you have.

t_ch: 能答应我一个要求吗？请你为我调杯饮料吧。
t_en: Will you do something for me? Please make me a drink.

t_ch: 做了这么久的调饮师，你心里应该也有数了——客人愿意坐在你的对面，就代表了对你有着绝对的信任。抱着朝九晚五的打工心态是绝对干不好这份工作的。
t_en: You've been a drinksmith for so long that you've probably figured it out. If a customer is sitting across from you, that means they absolutely trust you. You can't come at this job with a 9–5 mentality.

t_ch: 真期待啊…你会为我调出什么样的饮品呢？
t_en: I can't wait to see... what kind of drink you make me.

t_ch: （解决了黑桃和司令的情绪问题，它们以后应该能在酒吧和睦相处了吧？）
t_en: (After solving the issue between Spade and Mr. Bigwig, the two of them will probably get along peacefully at the lounge now, right?)

t_ch: （没想到舒翁曾经是「美梦剧团」的管理员，难怪她会如此在乎怪物们……）
t_en: (Siobhan was once the administrator for the Sweet Dreams Troupe. How unexpected. No wonder she has such a soft spot for monsters...)

t_ch: 和我想要的不太一样，但作为自由调制的教学而言，也算是足够了。
t_en: It's not exactly what I wanted, but it's good enough for a Free Mixing tutorial.

t_ch: 有空的时候就多练练吧，随着你的调饮技术越发熟练，说不定，有天我都得请你为我调制适合的饮品。
t_en: Do practice whenever you have free time. As your mixing skills get better and better, I might ask you to make a drink for me one day.

t_ch: （来了些对怪物们感兴趣的客人，酒吧也开始变得热闹了。）
t_en: (Some customers who are into monsters come in, and the lounge livens up.)

t_ch: （舒翁还是没出现…还是先开始营业吧。）
t_en: (Siobhan hasn't shown up yet... Well, let's start serving customers anyway.)

t_ch: （…约好了要聊聊，那个闹钟应该还在酒吧里等我吧？）
t_en: (I have a meeting to attend... Is that alarm clock waiting for me at the lounge?)

t_ch: 从吧台走过来就这几步路，至于花这么久？
t_en: The lounge is just a few steps away. What took you so long?

t_ch: 要是喊你加水，岂不是得等到嗓子冒烟才能喝到？我这都显示得清清楚楚，比标准晚了半个系统时，杯子里的冰块都化得差不多了！
t_en: If I want to get a refill, do I have to wait forever? Check out my watch! You're half an hour behind schedule, and the ice in my glass has already melted!

t_ch: …有这么长时间？
t_en: ...Has it really been that long?

t_ch: 你的表有问题吧？
t_en: Is something wrong with your timing?

t_ch: 这是你该问的问题吗？客人的话就是对的，要是连这点都做不到，就别站在那了。
t_en: Is that what you should be asking? The customer is always right, and if you can't even get that, then why are you even here?

t_ch: 真不知道舒翁是怎么教的，让我来给你补补课！
t_en: Honestly, I have no idea what Siobhan taught you. Let me fill you in!
...Phew! ...Phew!

t_ch: …怎么样？嘀嗒嘀阁下？
t_en: ...How's it going, Lord Tik-Tok?

t_ch: 你的这个梦还挺丰富呀。
t_en: Your dream is seriously wild.

t_ch: 给您道歉了——对不起！！！！！
t_en: It's all my fault... I'm so sorry!!!!

t_ch: 您调制的饮料非常美味，接待也很热情，我超爱这里的，以后还会常来玩！
t_en: The drink you made was tasty, and the whole vibe here was awesome! I'm totally loving this place, and I'll definitely come back again!

t_ch: 现在就回去了！告辞！
t_en: Well, I'm taking my leave! See you!

t_ch: （…至于这么害怕吗？）
t_en: (...What's making him so scared?)

t_ch: （有点不放心，还是跟出去看看吧。）
t_en: (Something's not right. I should follow him outside and check things out.)

t_ch: 汪！我才刚捡到新的收音机，你别过来哇！
t_en: Woof! Be careful with the new radio I just found! Don't even think about touching it!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎，有什么关系嘛。你看，我的手就这么大，力气也小，不会弄坏的。
t_en: Mwahaha, don't worry. Look, my hands are puny and feeble. I won't wreck it.

t_ch: 我也想听听听你说的声音嘛。嘻嘻，就一小会，行不？
t_en: I want to hear the "sound" you mentioned, too. Hee-hee, it'll be really quick, okay?

t_ch: 你们好，我是专程来……
t_en: Hey there, I've come all the way to...

t_ch: 汪！有更大的坏蛋靠近了哇！
t_en: Woof! A badder baddy is coming!

t_ch: <size=28>以前我太不懂事，给你们添了不少麻烦。对不起！！！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I used to be such an annoying troublemaker. I'm so sorry!!!</size>

t_ch: …汪？我听错了？
t_en: ...Woof? Did I hear that right?

t_ch: 那么大的个子，唯独没有脑子！嘿，别管它了，咱们继续玩！
t_en: That big guy doesn't have a brain, and that's for sure! Let's leave him be and have fun!

t_ch: （看起来它的问题暂时解决了，希望以后也能和其他怪物和睦相处吧。）
t_en: (Looks like his problem is resolved for now. Let's hope he gets along with the other monsters in the future, too.)

t_ch: 准备好了没？连个酒吧服务员的工作都做不好，就让我来给你上一课！
t_en: Are you ready? You can't even handle your job as a drinksmith! Let me show you how it's done!

t_ch: 给我看看呗。
t_en: Show me what you've got.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 哼，赶快处理完，别让你的客人等太久。
t_en: Hmph, hurry up and finish this. Don't keep your customers waiting.

t_ch: （调饮师的工作做久了还挺累的，舒翁是怎么坚持这么久的？）
t_en: (Being a drinksmith is actually exhausting. How did Siobhan manage to keep at it for so long?)

t_ch: （咦，它们还留在酒吧里……）
t_en: (Huh? They're still at the lounge...)

t_ch: 您好呀。有点难以启齿，但…人家有点事想找您帮忙。
t_en: Well, hey there. It's a bit awkward, but... I need to ask you a favor.

t_ch: 直说吧，我也很忙的。
t_en: Shoot. I don't have all day.

t_ch: 这件事…呀，要是直说就太丢人了。还是请您耐心点吧~
t_en: Well... it's really embarrassing to just say it outright. Please, be more patient.

t_ch: 我是最贴心的调饮师。
t_en: I'm the most caring drinksmith here.

t_ch: 就喜欢您这样温柔的类型~哎呀，要是*那位*也能这么贴心就好了。
t_en: Oh, I'm really fond of your gentle type! Alas, if only *he* could be as caring as you are.

t_ch: 就是…就是……
t_en: It's... well... it's...

t_ch: 我也不知道为什么喊我留在这里，就很郁闷，为什么，为什么就只针对我呢。
t_en: I have no idea why she asked me to stay here. This is just depressing. Why is it always me?

t_ch: 酒吧里明明还有那么多怪物，打烊后单独约我到角落里，是要欺凌我吧？然后明天会到处宣传我的糗事，联合同伴排挤我。
t_en: There are so many other monsters, but she asked me to sit alone in this corner after business hours. Is she trying to bully me or something? And tomorrow she'll go around spreading my embarrassing story and ostracize me, right?

t_ch: 什么…「不会吧，不会真的有人以为收到纸条是要向你表白吧？」
t_en: Like... "Come on! You actually thought receiving a note meant someone was confessing their love to you!?"

t_ch: 或者…「那个傻瓜真信了耶，你没看见当时的表情，又期待又害怕，笑死人了！」。
t_en: Or... "That fool actually fell for it! Did you see the nervous and expectant look on his face? Priceless!"

t_ch: 唉，开这种玩笑有意思吗……
t_en: *sigh* What's the point of making such jokes...?

t_ch: 才不是欺负你呢！人家是想…想要和你……
t_en: I'm not trying to bully you! I... I just want to...

t_ch: 呜哇哇哇哇！
t_en: Waaaah!

t_ch: （到底发生了什么…分别去问问它们的想法吧。）
t_en: (What's going on here...? I should talk to them separately and find out what they really think.)

t_ch: 就是…就是…唔……
t_en: It's... it's... you know...

t_ch: 人家…想约它出去玩嘛，又不知道…该怎么开口。
t_en: I... I wanted to ask him to hang out, but I don't know... how to go about it.

t_ch: 先前不是挺会说话的嘛？
t_en: I thought you were good at talking.

t_ch: 你直接说实话就行了吧。
t_en: Just be honest and tell him how you feel.

t_ch: 那是对不感兴趣的人呀，要是真在意…那种难为情的话怎么说得出口呢。
t_en: I'm good at chatting with people I don't care about. But when it comes to someone I do... how can I say something so embarrassing?

t_ch: 呜咕，我的心好痛。
t_en: Oh, what a heartrending story.

t_ch: …那我呢？
t_en: ...What about me?

t_ch: 呀！莫非您是吃醋了？
t_en: Yikes! Are you being jealous or something?

t_ch: 您想想，这儿所有的怪物都很喜欢您，一下子收获这么多倍的快乐，不也挺好吗？
t_en: Think about it, all the monsters here like you. Isn't it amazing to have so much happiness all at once?

t_ch: 人家就是…唉，平日里让人面红耳赤的话说惯了，到了关键时候，真心的话儿就怎么都没法坦率说出来。
t_en: I'm just... well, I'm just used to saying things that make people blush, but when it comes to the moment of truth, I just can't speak out what I really want to say.

t_ch: 呀！人家的真心…被您给找到了呢。
t_en: Awww! You've found... my true heart.

t_ch: 好了，别装了吧。
t_en: Alright, just stop pretending.

t_ch: 明明这么纯情…
t_en: You're pure and innocent deep down...

t_ch: 要是随便将心里话讲出来，可是很容易受伤的。
t_en: It's easy to get hurt if you just tell others what's in your heart.

t_ch: 不过还是要感谢您，发现了我真正的心意。这下可没法再逃避了呀。
t_en: Anyway, thanks for helping me figure out my true feelings. I won't run away anymore.

t_ch: 感谢您发现了人家的真实想法。这下可没法再逃避了呀。
t_en: Thank you for helping me realize my true feelings. I won't run away anymore.

t_ch: 唉，就很郁闷。为什么非要捉弄我呢，这世界上的伤心事还少吗？
t_en: It's so frustrating. Why do they have to make fun of me? Isn't there already enough sadness in the world?

t_ch: 不少吗？我原本就无依无靠，好不容易找到个安稳的地方，现在还要欺负我……
t_en: What a world. I had nowhere to go, and now that I've finally found a peaceful place, they want to bully me...

t_ch: 呜呜…哇！！！
t_en: W—Waaaah!!!

t_ch: 别哭了别哭了…
t_en: There, there...

t_ch: 叫你别哭了！！！
t_en: Stop crying already!!!

t_ch: 呜呜！！我知道这样很丢脸…可悲伤它始终围绕着我，我控制不住自己呜哇哇哇！
t_en: *sob* I know it's awkward... but I just can't shake off my grief... Waaaaah!

t_ch: （得先想办法让它冷静下来，去它的内心世界里看看吧。）
t_en: (I've got to figure out a way to calm him down... Let's delve into his inner world...)

t_ch: 咦，好像有人过来了……
t_en: Huh? Someone is coming over...

t_ch: …我在这呢。
t_en: ...It's me.

t_ch: 这不是来找你了嘛。
t_en: See? I'm coming for you.

t_ch: …调饮师{F#小姐}{M#先生}，你是怎么到这的？
t_en: ...{F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} Drinksmith? How did you get here?

t_ch: 我会神奇的魔法。
t_en: I work in mysterious ways.

t_ch: 不管在哪，我都会找到你。
t_en: Wherever you are, I'll find you.

t_ch: 普普通通的你，却是我的超人！我太感动了！呜呜…呜哇哇哇哇哇！！
t_en: You, an ordinary person... You're my superhero! It's so touching! Waa... Waaaaahhhh!!!

t_ch: 我好像…没那么伤心了。你看，我的翅膀都开始跳舞了。
t_en: I'm not feeling... so sorrowful anymore. Look, even my wings are starting to flutter.

t_ch: 自从遇到了你以后，感觉生活充满了快乐。悲剧终于要闭幕，欢笑的喜剧要上演了！
t_en: Life feels full of joy since I met you. The tragedy is finally ending, and the comedy is about to begin!

t_ch: 别忘了还有人在等你。
t_en: Don't forget that someone is waiting for you.

t_ch: …有、有嘛？！这种事情怎么可能……
t_en: ...R—Really!? But how can that be...?

t_ch: 那就开始新的生活吧。
t_en: Embrace your new life, then.

t_ch: 话说淑女说有事找我，该不会是想要欺……
t_en: By the way, Lady said she had something for me. She wouldn't be trying to bully...

t_ch: 不对不对，要更积极地思考，要时刻告诉自己，所有人都很喜欢我！
t_en: No, no, I should stay positive! I've got to remind myself that everyone likes me!

t_ch: （倒也不必到这种程度吧……）
t_en: (Maybe not to that extent...)

t_ch: 嗯！向着甜蜜的未来——出发！
t_en: Yeah! Let's go! Toward a sweet future!

t_ch: 自从遇到了你以后，感觉生活充满了快乐。我要更积极地生活！
t_en: Life feels full of joy since I met you. I want to be more positive!

t_ch: 那人家就出门了哦？
t_en: Then I'll take my leave, alright?

t_ch: 谢谢！你是真正的好人！
t_en: Thank you! You've been so kind!

t_ch: 去吧去吧。
t_en: Go ahead.

t_ch: 拜托别在我面前秀了。
t_en: Alright, enough with the public display of affection.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，像您这样温柔体贴的人，到哪都会受欢迎的。觉得孤独的话，下次我约您出去玩呀。
t_en: Hee-hee, a gentle person like you will be popular wherever you go. If you ever feel lonely, I'll ask you out next time.

t_ch: 不过，现在请先祝福我们吧~
t_en: But for now, please give us your blessing.

t_ch: 人家没有邀请你……
t_en: I'm not inviting you...

t_ch: <size=28>呀！来、来人啊——快救救我们！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Yikes! Please, s—someone! Help us!!</size>

t_ch: （求救声…是从酒吧外面传来的？）
t_en: (That cry for help... is coming from outside the lounge?)

t_ch: 酒吧还未恢复平静多久，不远处突然传来了呼救的声音。
t_en: Not long after the lounge had returned to calmness, cries for help suddenly came from nearby.

t_ch: 呜…呜啊啊啊！我还不想死！明明才下定决心以后要快乐生活，我才不要……
t_en: Waaa... Waaaahhhh! I don't want to die yet! I just decided to live a happy life from now on! I don't want...

t_ch: 亲、亲爱的别怕…人…人家会保护你……
t_en: C—Calm down, darling... I... I'll protect you...

t_ch: <size=28>吼——！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Roarrr!!!</size>

t_ch: 汪！醒醒哇。你、你认不出我们了吗？
t_en: Woof! Get back to your senses! You... you don't recognize us?

t_ch: <size=36>吼——！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Roarrr!!!</size>

t_ch: 汪！我不会让你伤害它们……
t_en: Woof! I won't let you harm them...

t_ch: 新来的调饮师，快来帮忙哇——小星星失去理智了汪！
t_en: Come help, new drinksmith! Starlet has lost his mind!

t_ch: 汪！你醒醒！你怎么了哇！
t_en: Woof! Wake up! What happened?

t_ch: 怎会如此？怎会如此！就连我们之中最智慧的思考者都变成了这副模样！
t_en: How could this be? How!? Even the wisest thinker among us has lost his sanity!

t_ch: 果然欢喜瞬息即逝、当真悲苦方为永恒——谁能告诉我，啊！既然注定要走向毁灭，如我们这般存在，又为何要降生于这不真切的「美梦」之中……
t_en: As I thought, it is indeed that happiness is transient while sorrow seems everlasting... Who can tell me... if we are doomed for ruin, why were we brought into this illusory "sweet dream" in the first place...?

t_ch: （上次见面时还好好的，怎么突然就发狂了？）
t_en: (He was fine the last time we met. Why did he suddenly become frenzied?)

t_ch: （得尽快通知舒翁……）
t_en: (I should inform Siobhan as soon as possible...)

t_ch: 怎么办哇？新来的调饮师，你那么聪明，一定有办法的吧！
t_en: What should we do? You're a smart one, new drinksmith! So there must be something you can do!

t_ch: 先把它带回酒吧。
t_en: Let's bring him back to the lounge first.

t_ch: 汪！我来帮你！
t_en: Woof! I'll lend a paw!

t_ch: 汪！看起来没事了。明明平时都有来酒吧，为什么会突然失去理智哇！
t_en: Woof! Looks like he's okay. He's a regular at the lounge, but why did he lose his mind all of a sudden?

t_ch: 人家不会有天也变成那样吧，好可怕呀。
t_en: Will I end up like that someday? That's scary.

t_ch: 唉，我们终将抵达命定的终点。
t_en: *sigh* We all meet our destined end someday.

t_ch: 汪…为什么这种时候，舒翁偏偏不在哇。
t_en: Woof... why isn't Siobhan here at a time like this?

t_ch: 她到底在做什么汪……
t_en: What is she doing, woof...?

t_ch: 我已经通知她了。
t_en: I've told her about it.

t_ch: 我会陪着你们的。
t_en: I'll stay with you guys.

t_ch: 呜…呜呜……
我们就先回去了汪。新来的调饮师…你也要好好保护自己哇。
t_en: We'll go back for now, woof. As for you, new drinksmith... stay safe, alright?

t_ch: 怪物们纷纷离开了酒吧，没过多久……
t_en: The monsters all left the lounge one after another, and not long after that...

t_ch: …我收到你的消息了。怪物们还好吗？
t_en: ...I've received your message. How are the monsters doing?

t_ch: 你向舒翁讲述了在梦境酒店里遇见的事。当说起那些发狂的怪物时，你注意到她似乎并未感到惊讶，平静的神色间夹着几分疲惫，仿佛对现状早已知晓。
t_en: You tell Siobhan about what happened in the hotel in the Dreamscape. When you mention the frenzied monsters, you notice that she doesn't seem all that surprised. Instead, a touch of fatigue appears on her calm face, as if she has been expecting it.

t_ch: 我刚才查看了它的情况，没什么大问题，过段时间就能恢复清醒。
t_en: I just checked on him, nothing serious. He'll regain his senses soon.

t_ch: …抱歉，我没想到会发生这种事。辛苦你了。
t_en: ...I'm sorry. I didn't see it coming. Thank you for your help.

t_ch: 你…是不是有事情瞒着我？
t_en: Are you... hiding something from me?

t_ch: 别紧张。我才刚从外面回来，有些累了。你不是个熟练的调饮师嘛，给我调杯饮料呗？
t_en: Chill out. I just came back, and I'm a bit tired. You're a skilled drinksmith, so how about whipping up a drink for me?

t_ch: 调饮的手艺进步不小嘛，说不定都可以把这家酒吧整个托付给你了。
t_en: You've gotten so good at mixing drinks. Maybe I can trust you with this lounge.

t_ch: 随着梦境加速坍塌，怪物发狂的情况会越来越多。我们调制的这些饮品，充其量只能暂时安抚它们的情绪，不知道什么时候是个头啊。
t_en: As the Dreamscape collapses faster, more monsters will go frenzied. The drinks we whip up will only calm them down temporarily, and we have no idea when this whole mess will end.

t_ch: 觉得很累么？
t_en: Are you feeling tired?

t_ch: 或许吧。有时候也止不住地会去想，自己究竟想做的是什么。
t_en: Perhaps. Sometimes I can't help but wonder what my true desire is.

t_ch: 但怪物们很需要你。
t_en: But those monsters need you.

t_ch: 是么？但…说实话，这或许只是你的看法。
t_en: Oh, really? But... to be honest, maybe that's just your opinion.

t_ch: 即使是在梦里，生活也不会那么单纯，努力并非一定能得到结果，这世界总是这样。
t_en: Life isn't simple even in a dream, and hard work doesn't always pay off. That's just how the world works.

t_ch: 我还有些必须解决的麻烦事，那群孩子就拜托你了。
t_en: I've got some troubles I need to sort out. Please take care of those kids while I'm gone.

t_ch: 希望…你能喜欢上这里。
t_en: I hope... you enjoy it here.

t_ch: 汪！…这边，到这边来，有点事情想和你商量哇！
t_en: Woof! ...This way, come this way. I've got something to talk to you about!

t_ch: 汪！你不觉得舒翁最近的行为很可疑嘛，成天不在酒吧，到底去哪了哇！
t_en: Woof! Don't you think Siobhan has been acting suspiciously lately? She's never around the lounge these days. Where has she gone?

t_ch: 确实很可疑。
t_en: Suspicious indeed.

t_ch: 我也很好奇。
t_en: I'm curious, too.

t_ch: 果然你和我们想得一样哇！
t_en: I knew you'd feel the same way!

t_ch: 你也知道，舒翁是个很厉害的人，然后我们就在想……
t_en: As you know, Siobhan is an amazing woman, so we've been wondering...

t_ch: 要是舒翁这么厉害，最近又常常不在，会不会…她不想要我们了哇？
t_en: Since she's been away a lot lately, could it be that... she doesn't want us anymore?

t_ch: 最近大家的情绪不太好，惹了不少麻烦，会不会…是被她讨厌了哇？
t_en: We've been a pain in the neck lately and have caused a lot of trouble. Maybe... she's not into us anymore?

t_ch: 她总是说有事情要忙，却从来不肯告诉我们细节……
t_en: She always says she's busy with something, but never spills the beans...

t_ch: （仔细想想，舒翁确实从来没有透露过自己在忙些什么。）
t_en: (Come to think of it, Siobhan never mentioned what she's been busy with.)

t_ch: 新来的调饮师！我们去外面找舒翁吧！要是能帮上她的忙就更好了汪！
t_en: Hey, new drinksmith! Let's go outside and find Siobhan! It'd be great if we could help her out! Woof!

t_ch: 该去哪里找呢？
t_en: Where should we start?

t_ch: 汪汪！我先去做准备！调查舒翁可能会在的地方哇！
t_en: Woof! Let me make some preparations and figure out where Siobhan might be!

t_ch: 我需要先处理完酒吧的工作。
t_en: I need to finish my work at the lounge first.

t_ch: 汪！没问题哇！我先去做准备，调查舒翁可能会在的地方哇！
t_en: Woof! No problem! Let me make some preparations and figure out where Siobhan might be!

t_ch: 就这么说定了汪！
t_en: It's a deal! Woof!

t_ch: （约好了要去找舒翁，不过得先把酒吧的事忙完……）
t_en: (I've promised him to look for Siobhan, but I need to finish the lounge work first...)

t_ch: （没记错的话，应该只有小星星的问题还没解决了吧？）
t_en: (If I remember correctly, Starlet is the only one who is still troubled by his problem, right?)

t_ch: …最真实的想法，究竟是什么呢？
t_en: ...What are my true feelings?

t_ch: 要是轻易就把真心表露出来…不是会显得太急躁了吗？
t_en: If I reveal my true feelings so easily... won't I seem too impatient?

t_ch: 猜呀，猜呀，怎么都猜不透…想要再一次确定……
t_en: I keep wondering but never get an answer... I want to be reassured...

t_ch: （…那个闹钟先前这么威风，情绪平静下来后还是挺懂事的。）
t_en: (...That alarm clock was a pain, but now he seems quite prudent when he is calmed down.)

t_ch: （准备得差不多，该开始营业了。）
t_en: (Almost ready. Time to open for business.)

t_ch: 我说我说，您不是有些特别厉害的能力嘛？能不能麻烦您进到人家的心里，看看到底是哪里堵住了呢？
t_en: Hey, there? Don't you have some awesome powers? Could you dive into my heart and see what is blocking me?

t_ch: 我来帮你解决烦恼。
t_en: Sure, I'll help you solve your problems.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 您先去忙吧。人家就在这等着。
t_en: Please go ahead. I'll be waiting here.

t_ch: 没有人会喜欢，我只是一个笑柄…好冷，我好孤独……
t_en: Nobody likes me. I'm just a standing joke... I'm feeling so cold, so lonely...

t_ch: 我试过销声匿迹…却发现根本无人问津…
t_en: I've tried to disappear... only to realize that no one cares at all...

t_ch: 呜呜！！我知道这样很丢脸…可悲伤它始终围绕着我，我控制不住自己呜哇哇哇！
t_en: *sob* I know it's awkward... but I just can't shake off my grief... Waaaaah!

t_ch: 别怕，我来帮你。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll help you.

t_ch: 我还有别的事。
t_en: I've got something else to do.

t_ch: 呜呜呜呜呜呜。你去吧，就让我在这自生自灭好了。
t_en: Boohoooo... Just go ahead and leave me be.

t_ch: （淑女和忧郁派的关系还真好啊，喜欢它们表演的人挺多的嘛。）
t_en: (Lady and Melancholy are really close. Lots of people are fond of their performances.)

t_ch: （不知道小星星恢复得怎么样，它今天还会来酒吧吗？）
t_en: (I wonder how Starlet is doing. Will he show up today?)

t_ch: （…有点好奇，不过还是先开始营业吧。）
t_en: (...As curious as I am, I should get the business started first.)

t_ch: （结束了手头的事，就去看看舒翁究竟在忙什么吧。）
t_en: (I'll go see what Siobhan is up to when I'm done here.)

t_ch: （现在要先去和小星星聊聊。）
t_en: (Now, I need to have a chat with Starlet.)

t_ch: 你还是来了。
t_en: You've come after all.

t_ch: 开始吧。反复辩论没有意义，只有事实才能指明方向。
t_en: Let's get it started. There's no point in repeated arguments, as only facts can guide us to the path.

t_ch: 我为你准备了用于模拟的思维空间，看清楚了——我们，所谓的「美梦剧团」究竟过着怎样的生活。
t_en: I've prepared a Mindspace for simulations. Watch closely and see what kind of life we Sweet Dreams Troupe members are actually living.

t_ch: 如果你站在道路的分岔点，又会如何选择呢？
t_en: What would you choose if you were the one at the crossroads?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 不用着急，我就在这等你。
t_en: No need to rush. I'll be here waiting for you.

t_ch: 做好进入特殊空间的准备了么？在那里你可以看到——我们，所谓的「美梦剧团」——究竟过着怎样的生活。
t_en: Ready to dive into the special space? There, you'll find out what kind of life we so-called Sweet Dreams Troupe members are actually living.

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 不用着急，我就在这等你。
t_en: No need to rush. I'll be here waiting for you.

t_ch: （做这个梦可比战斗要累多了……）
t_en: (Diving into this dream is much more tiring than a fight...)

t_ch: 舒翁说的没错，我承认你是一位极为优秀的调饮师。
t_en: Siobhan was right. Now I admit that you're an extraordinary drinksmith.

t_ch: 你很适合「惊梦酒吧」。我代表所有成员向你表示欢迎。
t_en: You're a perfect fit for Dreamjolt Hostelry. On behalf of all the members, welcome aboard.

t_ch: 这段时间辛苦了。我还会来光顾的。
t_en: Thank you for what you've done. I'll drop by next time.

t_ch: 感谢你的付出。
t_en: Thank you for everything.

t_ch: （好了，现在怪物们的问题都得到了解决…该出发去调查舒翁了！）
t_en: (Now that I've resolved all the monsters' problems... it's time to investigate Siobhan!)

t_ch: 汪！来了哇，等你好久了！
t_en: Woof! There you are! I've been waiting for you!

t_ch: 我和它们都说过了，不会过来酒吧哇，我们可以自由行动！
t_en: I told them that we wouldn't be at the lounge today! Now we can do whatever we want!

t_ch: 可以，你有什么计划？
t_en: Sounds great. What's your plan?

t_ch: 我们是不是得先调查…
t_en: Let's focus on our investigation...

t_ch: 放心吧，汽水狗狗永远是你最可靠的伙伴！
t_en: No worries! Soda Doggo will always be your most reliable partner!

t_ch: 虽然我从来没离开过酒店，但我帅气又灵敏的鼻子会带我们找到舒翁的汪！
t_en: Although I've never left the hotel, my handsome and keen nose will lead us straight to Siobhan! Woof!

t_ch: 汪汪！听说那个「黄金的时刻」特别大、人也特别多，你可要跟紧了，别走丢了哇！
t_en: Woof! I hear that Golden Hour is huge and crowded, so stick close and don't get lost!

t_ch: 走这边，汪！
t_en: This way, woof!

t_ch: 汪。闻到了奇怪的味道……
t_en: Woof! I'm smelling something weird...

t_ch: 你们——在大街上遛什么狗呢？跑得还挺欢，嘻嘻，我看是狗在遛你才对吧！
t_en: Hey, guys... walking your dog, huh? He looks so happy, hee-hee! You know, I think he's the one taking you for a walk!

t_ch: …你怎么在这？
t_en: ...What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我想在哪逛，你管得着嘛？再说，现在梦里乱成这样，好多烂摊子等着我收拾呢。
t_en: I'm going wherever I want! Plus, the Dreamscape is a mess, and I've got mountains of clean-up tasks to do.

t_ch: 我是不是在哪见过你？
t_en: Have we met somewhere?

t_ch: 咦，有吗？没有吧，我跟许多人都长得很像，肯定是你记错了。
t_en: Huh? Nah, I don't think so. I look like a lot of people, so you must be mistaking me for someone else.

t_ch: 汪！我们是有很重要的事要去做哇！
t_en: Woof! We've got something super important to do!

t_ch: 知道啦知道啦。会说话的狗狗，真可爱。下次再给你带礼物哦。我还有事要忙，先走啦。
t_en: Yeah, yeah. Talking dog, so cute. I'll bring you a gift next time, but for now, I've got work to do, so I'm outta here.

t_ch: 汪！闪闪亮亮的！好多人！外面原来这么热闹哇！
t_en: Woof! Look at all the shiny stuff! And so many people! It's so lively out there!

t_ch: 汪！这就是钟表小子哇！这里好宽敞，特别适合玩飞盘！
t_en: Woof! So that's Clockie! This place is so spacious, perfect for a game of floatdisc!

t_ch: 差点忘了正事！我、我们还是先去找舒翁哇！呜汪…这里气味有点杂……
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot! L—Let's find Siobhan first! Woof... so many scents mixed up here...

t_ch: …是在找我吗？
t_en: ...Are you looking for me?

t_ch: 别紧张，开个玩笑而已。你似乎有些烦恼，让我为你抽张牌如何？
t_en: Chill out. Just kidding. You seem a little troubled. How about I draw a fortune card for you?

t_ch: …是什么意思？
t_en: ...What does that mean?

t_ch: 你打算做什么？
t_en: What's your plan?

t_ch: 就是顺手帮你个忙，稍微还点人情罢了。呵呵，完成了。
t_en: Just doing you a favor and returning the favor. Well, it's done.

t_ch: 汪！我找到舒翁的味道了，走走！
t_en: Woof! I've caught Siobhan's scent! Let's go!

t_ch: 快去吧。时机这种东西，若是不好好把握，可是会溜走的。
t_en: Now, go. Seize the opportunity before it slips away.

t_ch: 汪！快看，是舒翁！旁边那个人好像在哪见……
t_en: Woof! Look, it's Siobhan! And the person next to her... I think I've met her before...

t_ch: 是那个想把舒翁从酒吧带走的坏人哇！她们怎么会在一起！
t_en: Hey, it's the baddie who tried to take Siobhan away from the lounge! How did they end up together!?

t_ch: 汪汪汪——我去把舒翁拽回来！
t_en: Woof, woof! I'll drag Siobhan back!
